title: Malope lutea
taxonomy: Malope lutea is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Malope, which consists of a few annual or biennial species. The species Malope lutea is native to Europe and Western Asia, and is also naturalized in other parts of the world. Malope lutea is an annual plant that grows to a height of around 1-3 feet, with yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Its classification is further supported by its botanical name, which reflects its relationships within the Malvaceae family.
uses: Malope lutea, also known as the dwarf mallow, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related conditions. In some cultures, it's used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea. Additionally, Malope lutea is believed to have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in wound care and preventing infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses and to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties.

intro: Malope lutea, also known as hollyhock mallow, is a flowering plant that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The leaves and flowers of Malope lutea have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and soothing digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Malope lutea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
